title: 1A PASS: No hardcoded stream name literals in production source code
evidence: Grep for stream:[a-z-]+ in services/**/*.ts (excluding __tests__) returned only comment lines. All functional stream name arguments use RedisStreams.* constants from @arbitrage/types.
expected: All stream name arguments use RedisStreams constants
actual: Confirmed — no hardcoded stream name string literals in production code paths
recommendation: No action required. Continue enforcing via code review.

title: 1B PASS: All consumer group names match system inventory
evidence: Found groups: coordinator-group (coordinator.ts:394), cross-chain-detector-group (detector.ts:331,344,351), execution-engine-group (opportunity.consumer.ts:168 and fast-lane.consumer.ts:102 — reuses group per CA-005 fix), mempool-detector-group (mempool-config.ts:77), orderflow-pipeline (orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts:85), failover-{serviceName} dynamic (cross-region-health.ts:750).
actual: All 6 expected group patterns present. No unknown or rogue groups found.

title: 1C PASS: All 24 streams have MAXLEN; no raw xadd bypasses found
evidence: STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS in shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:402-430 defines 24 entries matching all 24 RedisStreams constants. HMAC DLQ path at line 883 now uses xaddWithLimit (fixed from raw xadd). No direct redis.xadd() bypasses found outside the RedisStreamsClient abstraction.
expected: All 24 streams covered by MAXLEN config; no raw xadd bypasses
actual: Full coverage confirmed. DLQ path fix verified.

title: 1D PASS: XACK present in all consumer-group files
evidence: Files with xReadGroup: shared/core/src/redis/stream-consumer.ts (xack at lines 262, 371), shared/core/src/analytics/orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts (xack at line 346), shared/core/src/monitoring/cross-region-health.ts (xack at line 808). All services use StreamConsumer class which handles ACK internally.
expected: All consumer-group consumers also ACK messages
actual: All files either directly xack or delegate to StreamConsumer class. No missing ACK found.

title: 1E: WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY and chain-specific private key vars not documented in .env.example
evidence: WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY used in shared/config/src/ and execution-engine initialization. ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BSC_PRIVATE_KEY, ARBITRUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BASE_PRIVATE_KEY, POLYGON_PRIVATE_KEY used in execution-engine. All absent from .env.example. Operators have no documented path for configuring private keys.
expected: All *_PRIVATE_KEY vars documented in .env.example with NEVER_COMMIT warnings and .env.local guidance
actual: Private key vars absent from .env.example — critical for production deployment, security-sensitive
recommendation: Add all *_PRIVATE_KEY vars to .env.example with explicit comment: # SECURITY: Never commit. Place ONLY in .env.local (gitignored). Generate with: node -e "require('ethers').Wallet.createRandom().privateKey"

title: 1E: 17 behavior-controlling env vars undocumented in .env.example
evidence: Undocumented behavior-controlling vars found across services: OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME, OTEL_BATCH_SIZE, OTEL_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS (shared/core/src/tracing/), DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN (coordinator dashboard routes), CIRCUIT_BREAKER_API_KEY (execution-engine circuit-breaker-api.ts), TRADE_LOG_ENABLED, TRADE_LOG_DIR (engine.ts:385-386), GAS_PRICE_MANTLE_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_MODE_GWEI (gas-price-optimizer.ts), JWT_SECRET, JWT_EXPIRES_IN (shared/security/src/auth.ts), BCRYPT_ROUNDS, RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS, EXECUTION_STREAM_BACKPRESSURE_RATIO (coordinator.ts:422), BALANCE_MONITOR_LOW_THRESHOLD_ETH (engine.ts:673).
expected: All behavior-controlling env vars documented in .env.example with defaults and descriptions
actual: 17 vars absent — operators cannot configure these without reading source code
recommendation: Add all 17 vars to .env.example under appropriate sections. Priority: JWT_SECRET (auth fails without it), DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN and CIRCUIT_BREAKER_API_KEY (security), TRADE_LOG_ENABLED (operational visibility).

title: 1F PASS: No nullish coalescing anti-patterns (|| 0 / || 0n) found
evidence: Searched services/ and shared/ source files (excluding __tests__ and node_modules) for || 0 and || 0n patterns. Zero matches found. Code correctly uses ?? 0 / ?? 0n throughout.
actual: Confirmed clean. ESLint no-restricted-syntax rule is effective.

title: 1G PASS: HMAC signing documented in .env.example and fail-closed in production
evidence: .env.example: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY= documented with generation instructions; STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS= documented for key rotation. streams.ts constructor throws Error when signingKey is null in NODE_ENV=production (fail-closed). HMAC-SHA256 signing with crypto.timingSafeEqual verification confirmed in shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts.
actual: Both confirmed. HMAC enforcement is correct.

title: 1H PASS: All 23 FEATURE_* flags present and cross-dependencies validated
evidence: All 23 FEATURE_* flags confirmed: FEATURE_BATCHED_QUOTER, FEATURE_FLASH_LOAN_AGGREGATOR, FEATURE_COMMIT_REVEAL, FEATURE_COMMIT_REVEAL_REDIS, FEATURE_DEST_CHAIN_FLASH_LOAN, FEATURE_MOMENTUM_TRACKING, FEATURE_ML_SIGNAL_SCORING, FEATURE_SIGNAL_CACHE_READ, FEATURE_LIQUIDITY_DEPTH_SIZING, FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES, FEATURE_ORDERFLOW_PIPELINE, FEATURE_KMS_SIGNING, FEATURE_FAST_LANE, FEATURE_BACKRUN_STRATEGY, FEATURE_UNISWAPX_FILLER, FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN, FEATURE_SOLANA_EXECUTION, FEATURE_STATISTICAL_ARB, FEATURE_MEV_SHARE, FEATURE_ADAPTIVE_RISK_SCORING, FEATURE_TIMEBOOST, FEATURE_FLASHBOTS_PROTECT_L2, FEATURE_COW_BACKRUN. validateFeatureFlags() checks SIGNAL_CACHE_READ->ML_SIGNAL_SCORING, COMMIT_REVEAL_REDIS->REDIS_URL, SOLANA_EXECUTION->SOLANA_RPC_URL. FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES correctly opt-out (!== false).
expected: 23 flags, correct opt-in/opt-out patterns, cross-dep validation

title: 1I PASS: All risk config vars documented in .env.example with production requirements
evidence: .env.example lines 940-1005 documents RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL (noted as REQUIRED for production), RISK_KELLY_MULTIPLIER, RISK_MAX_SINGLE_TRADE, RISK_MIN_EV_THRESHOLD, RISK_MIN_WIN_PROBABILITY, RISK_MAX_LOSS_PER_TRADE. risk-config.ts validateRiskConfig() throws if defaultWinProbability < minWinProbability (cross-validation). All parses use safeParseFloatBounded/safeParseIntBounded (no raw parseInt).
expected: All RISK_* vars documented; cross-validation present

title: 1J PASS: All parseInt/parseFloat calls on env vars have NaN protection in critical paths
evidence: Changed files checked: engine.ts lines 395-407: MAX_CONCURRENT_EXECUTIONS uses Number.isNaN guard. engine.ts lines 368-377: AB_TESTING config uses full NaN validation. gas-price-optimizer.ts: validateGasPrice() wraps parseFloat with NaN check. shared/config/src/risk-config.ts: uses safeParseFloatBounded/safeParseIntBounded helpers. parsePort() helper used in unified-detector/src/constants.ts:61.
expected: All parseInt/parseFloat on env vars protected against NaN
actual: Critical paths confirmed protected. Uses safe utility helpers throughout.
recommendation: No action required for changed files.

title: 1K PASS: Redis client and streams client fully aligned — split-brain risk resolved
evidence: client.ts and streams.ts both have: retryStrategy returning never-null (retry forever) with Math.min(Math.round(200 * Math.pow(2, Math.min(times-1, 18))), 60000) capped exponential backoff; connectTimeout=5000; maxRetriesPerRequest=3; lazyConnect=true. Both log warn at attempt 1, every 5th attempt, error after 30 attempts. The v2.3 known split-brain issue has been resolved.
expected: Identical retry behavior, connectTimeout, maxRetriesPerRequest, lazyConnect
recommendation: No action required. Split-brain risk fully resolved.

title: 1L PASS: All service port assignments match service-ports.json registry
evidence: Registry: coordinator=3000, partition-asia-fast=3001, partition-l2-turbo=3002, partition-high-value=3003, partition-solana=3004, execution-engine=3005, cross-chain-detector=3006, unified-detector=3007, mempool-detector=3008. unified-detector/src/constants.ts DEFAULT_HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3007 (previously 3001 — conflict resolved). No duplicate ports. monolith uses 3100. No collisions.
expected: All service ports unique and match registry
actual: All ports match exactly. Port 3001 conflict in unified-detector was fixed (now correctly 3007).

title: 1M PASS: No empty catch blocks found in production code
evidence: Searched services/ and shared/ (excluding __tests__, .test.ts, .spec.ts) for catch\s*\{\s*\} and catch\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\{\s*\} patterns. All catch blocks either log the error, re-throw, or have an explanatory comment justifying silent swallowing. No unguarded empty catch in hot-path files (execution-engine, detection pipeline).
expected: No empty catch blocks without explanation in production code

title: 1N PASS: Stream serialization type fidelity maintained with correct round-trip
evidence: stream-serialization.ts:49-60 converts profitPercentage, confidence, expectedProfit, estimatedProfit via .toString(). validation.ts:327-337 restores all four via parseFloat() before the as-unknown-as cast. parseStreamResult in streams.ts:1395 also applies JSON.parse which correctly converts '0.5' to 0.5. Double-restore path is safe (parseFloat(0.5) = 0.5).
expected: Numeric fields serialized as strings must be parsed back to numbers
actual: Correct round-trip confirmed. No type fidelity loss.
recommendation: No action required. Defensive parseFloat in validation.ts is appropriate belt-and-suspenders.

title: 1O: No formal Redis key prefix registry document exists
evidence: Key prefixes found in code: 'lock:execution:', 'bridge:recovery:', 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:', 'commit-reveal:', 'price:', 'coordinator:leader:lock', 'risk:probabilities:', 'stream:', A/B test prefix (configurable via redisKeyPrefix). No docs/redis-key-registry.md or equivalent found. 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:' prefix is a substring of 'bridge:recovery:' — SCAN pattern 'bridge:recovery:*' matches both.
expected: Centralized Redis key registry documenting all prefixes, owners, TTL policies
actual: 9 key prefixes identified with no registry. Substring collision: bridge:recovery: SCAN captures bridge:recovery:corrupt: entries.
recommendation: Create docs/redis-key-registry.md documenting all prefixes. Change corrupt key prefix to 'bridge:corrupt:' to avoid SCAN collision with main recovery scanner.

title: 1P PASS: All ADR spot-checks confirmed compliant
evidence: ADR-022 (hot-path memory): No spread operators in loops in price-matrix.ts, partitioned-detector.ts, execution-engine/, unified-detector/. ADR-033 (stale price): maxPriceAgeMs default = 30000ms in cross-chain-detector/src/detector.ts:1458 (per ADR-033). ADR-002 (Redis Streams): No direct inter-service HTTP calls found (CORS allowlist entries are for browser dashboard, not inter-service RPC). ADR-018 (circuit breaker): failureThreshold default = 5 in execution-engine/src/types.ts:681 and coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:47. ADR-005 (hierarchical cache): SharedArrayBuffer confirmed as primary L1 storage in price-matrix.ts:224,438-450.

title: 1Q: Coordinator Fly.io TOML missing health check configuration block
evidence: infrastructure/fly/coordinator.toml has [http_service] with internal_port=3000 but NO [checks] or [http_service.checks] block. All other service TOMLs (partition-*, execution-engine, cross-chain-detector) have [checks.health] with path and interval configured. Fly.io will use default TCP check on port 3000 instead of HTTP /api/health — may pass TCP but fail application-level health.
expected: coordinator.toml has [checks.health] with path='/api/health' matching the coordinator's health endpoint
actual: No health check block in coordinator.toml. Fly.io TCP probe may mask application-level failures.
recommendation: Add to coordinator.toml: [[http_service.checks]] interval = '10s' timeout = '5s' grace_period = '30s' method = 'GET' path = '/api/health'

title: 1R: Cross-chain-detector SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS equals Redis connectTimeout (boundary condition)
evidence: services/cross-chain-detector/src/detector.ts:501 - SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms used for disconnectWithTimeout(). shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:490 - connectTimeout=5000ms. Both are exactly 5000ms. In practice disconnectWithTimeout() closes existing connections (lower latency than new connections), but under slow I/O the timeouts race.
expected: shutdown disconnect timeout > redis connectTimeout for clean separation
actual: Both are 5000ms (equal). Not a hard violation — closing existing connections differs from new connection latency — but a 3000ms margin would be safer.
recommendation: Consider increasing SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS to 8000ms in cross-chain-detector to provide buffer above the 5000ms Redis connectTimeout, especially in cloud environments with variable I/O latency.
